ddls:
CREATE TABLE "benchmark_tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benchmark test */,
  "test_name" TEXT /* Name of the benchmark test */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the benchmark test */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the test was created */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the benchmark category */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware device used for the test */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who conducted the test */,
  "software_version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the software version used for the test */,
  "test_duration" REAL /* Total duration of the test in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benchmark_tests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benchmark_tests_software_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("software_version_id") REFERENCES software_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benchmark_tests_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES device_info ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benchmark_tests_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES benchmark_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cache_configurations" (
  "cache_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cache configuration */,
  "cache_type" TEXT /* Type of cache (e.g., L1, L2, L3) */,
  "size" INTEGER /* Size of the cache in KB */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the cache configuration */,
  "line_size" INTEGER /* Size of each cache line in bytes */,
  "associativity" TEXT /* Type of cache associativity (e.g., direct-mapped, 2-way set associative) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cache_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the benchmark test conducted */,
  "cache_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the cache configuration used */,
  "data_size" REAL /* Size of the data being tested in KB */,
  "time_cyc" INTEGER /* Time taken in cycles for the benchmark */,
  "time_taken" REAL /* Total time taken for the test in seconds */,
  "bytes_transferred" REAL /* Total bytes transferred during the test */,
  "transfers" INTEGER /* Number of transfers made during the test */,
  "bandwidth" REAL /* Bandwidth measured in GB/s */,
  "transfers_per_second" REAL /* Number of transfers per second */,
  "latency" REAL /* Average latency in microseconds */,
  "error_rate" REAL /* Error rate during the test */,
  "throughput" REAL /* Throughput measured in MB/s */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_cache_id FOREIGN KEY ("cache_id") REFERENCES cache_configurations ("cache_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES benchmark_tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sizes" (
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for data size */,
  "size_value" INTEGER /* Value of the data size in KB */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data size */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the data size (e.g., KB, MB) */,
  "is_standard" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the data size is a standard size */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the system (e.g., tester, analyst, admin) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the access event */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., create, read, update, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the access request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result entry */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the benchmark test */,
  "cache_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the cache configuration used */,
  "average_bandwidth" REAL /* Average bandwidth measured during the test */,
  "max_bandwidth" REAL /* Maximum bandwidth achieved during the test */,
  "min_bandwidth" REAL /* Minimum bandwidth measured during the test */,
  "median_bandwidth" REAL /* Median bandwidth measured during the test */,
  "std_dev_bandwidth" REAL /* Standard deviation of the bandwidth */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_cache_id FOREIGN KEY ("cache_id") REFERENCES cache_configurations ("cache_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES benchmark_tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "benchmark_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benchmark category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Memory, Bandwidth) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to a parent category for hierarchical categorization */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the category was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benchmark_categories_parent_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_category_id") REFERENCES benchmark_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting entry */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the benchmark test */,
  "setting_key" TEXT /* Key for the setting (e.g., Cache Size, Data Type) */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value for the setting */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the setting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the setting was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_settings_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES benchmark_tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "software_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each software version */,
  "software_name" TEXT /* Name of the software used */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the software */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the software version */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the software version is the current version */,
  "changelog" TEXT /* Changelog or release notes for the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_logs" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error log entry */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the benchmark test that encountered the error */,
  "error_time" TEXT /* Date and time when the error occurred */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Description of the error */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the error (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the error was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES benchmark_tests ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_expiry" (
  "expiry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each expiry entry */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the benchmark test */,
  "expiry_date" TEXT /* Expiry date of the benchmark test results */,
  "is_expired" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the test results have expired */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("expiry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_expiry_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES benchmark_tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_info" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device (e.g., CPU, GPU) */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device */,
  "specification" TEXT /* Detailed specifications of the device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the device manufacturer */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the device */,
  "year_of_manufacture" INTEGER /* Year the device was manufactured */,
  "is_supported" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the device is supported by the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)
